channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: American woman held in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American woman detained for weeks in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released from custody, according to a person familiar with the matter and a social media post Saturday from a longtime U.S. diplomat.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A weekend ritual for Trump's Florida die-hards to get a brief glimpse of their political hero
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The sun was shining outside President Donald Trump's West Palm Beach golf course on Saturday morning when Alan Mentser got a call letting him know that police were shutting down a road nearby. It was almost time to “show the boss a little love.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to do before you quit your job
item.description: Thinking about changing jobs? You’re in good company. Job tenure is now the shortest it has been in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, NewsNation

item.title: Protesters rebelling against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds protesting billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump began amassing outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Critics see Trump attacks on the 'Black Smithsonian' as an effort to sanitize racism in US history
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump's order accusing the Smithsonian Institution of not reflecting American history notes correctly that the country's Founding Fathers declared that “all men are created equal.” But it doesn't mention that the founders enshrined slavery into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking ‘upskirt’ photos, lawsuit alleges
item.description: A new lawsuit alleges that Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking "upskirt" photos of guests at its Florida theme park in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KRON4

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said. Delta Air Lines Flight 2983 was cleared for takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Friday around 3:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin judge won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters. Attorney general appeals
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin judge on Saturday declined to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters in the state at a planned rally days before the closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Amanda Knox's advice for American linked to Punta Cana missing persons case
item.description: Amanda Knox was a student studying abroad in Italy when she was accused of murdering friend Meredith Kercher. She spent nearly four years in an Italian prison before she was exonerated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump administration cannot immediately deport Tufts student
item.description: A federal judge recently ruled a Tufts University grad student cannot be immediately deported. Meanwhile, ICE agents made their latest student detainment near the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Chicago VA nurses protest Trump cuts, warn of negative impact on veterans' care
item.description: With the expansion of coverage under the PACT Act, passed with bipartisan support, more veterans seek care.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck vandals caught on video scrawling 'Nazis' and swastika: NYPD
item.description: The New York Police Department Hate Crime Task Force is investigating after two men were caught on camera carving "Nazis" and a swastika onto a Cybertruck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Patient dies after getting rabies from organ transplant
item.description: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services spokesperson Lynn Sutfin said there is no threat to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, pushing past opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems that disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How marijuana could blaze a new path for movie theaters
item.description: Let's be blunt: movie joints need to find a new way to pipe in the patrons. A recent article from Variety's William Earl pondered the question on every film executive's mind: how do theaters lure back moviegoers? One suggestion: make them marijuana-friendly.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's anti-DEI executive orders
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked the U.S. Department of Labor from implementing parts of President Donald Trump’s executive orders aimed at curbing diversity, equity and inclusion efforts among federal contractors and grant recipients. Judge Matthew Kennelly of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE SAVAGE and ALEXANDRA OLSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and painting the word “resist” on the service center's doors has been arrested, police announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia mom’s Walmart trip devolves into ‘tug-of-warring’ in desperate attempt to save her son
item.description: A Georgia mother is in shock and a man is behind bars after he allegedly tried to grab a 2-year-old child while the family was shopping in a local Walmart.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mia Love's family announces funeral plans
item.description: The family of Mia Love has announced funeral plans for the former Utah congresswoman, who passed away Sunday, March 23, at the age of 49, after battling brain cancer.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
